hints.confortabatur: 'confortabatur' is a verb meaning 'was being strengthened'

hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'puerum' — 'Puer' is the nominative subject
hints.Vocative: Vocative would be 'puer' (same form), but here it is the subject of the verbs

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'pueri' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'pueri' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'pueri' ends in -i

hints.to increase (transitive): 'to increase' transitively is 'augere' — 'crescere' is intransitive (to grow)

hints.Present: Present would be 'crescit' — 'crescebat' with -ba- is imperfect
hints.Perfect: Perfect would be 'crevit' — 'crescebat' with -ba- is imperfect

hints.credo, credere, credidi, creditum: These are the parts of 'credere' (to believe)
hints.creo, creare, creavi, creatum: These are the parts of 'creare' (to create)
hints.cerno, cernere, crevi, cretum: These are the parts of 'cernere' (to discern) — same perfect stem but different verb

hints.to comfort: English 'comfort' derives from this word but Latin 'confortare' means 'to strengthen'
hints.to carry together: 'to carry together' is 'conferre' — 'confortare' means 'to make strong' (con + fortis)

hints.Present passive: Present passive would be 'confortatur' — the -ba- infix marks imperfect
hints.Perfect passive: Perfect passive would be 'confortatus est' — 'confortabatur' is imperfect passive
hints.Imperfect active: Imperfect active would be 'confortabat' — the -tur ending marks passive voice

hints.confero, conferre, contuli, collatum: These are the parts of 'conferre' (to bring together/compare)
hints.formo, formare, formavi, formatum: These are the parts of 'formare' (to form/shape)
hints.confirmo, confirmare, confirmavi, confir: These are the parts of 'confirmare' (to strengthen/confirm) — similar but different verb

translation: The boy was growing and was being strengthened
